sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 29, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves block right by ~5 cols (VERIFIED step 2)', 'LEFT': 'Moves block left by ~5 cols; works from rows 25-29 corridor opening but blocked from rows 30-44 (VERIFIED steps 6-22)', 'UP': 'Moves block up by 5 rows (VERIFIED step 4)', 'DOWN': 'Moves block down by 5 rows (VERIFIED step 8)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'white_cross': 'Static reference marker at left of gray room (rows ~31-33, cols ~19-22)', 'orange_blue_block': 'Movable object: 4x2 orange on top + 5x3 blue below, moves in 5-cell steps', 'dark_gray_corridor': 'L-shaped passage inside gray room: vertical segment at cols 29-33, horizontal at rows 40-44 cols 14-33', 'blue_templates': 'Two blue L-shapes in black frames (top-center and bottom-left) - purpose unknown'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'Yellow bar + red squares - likely timer/progress'}
Current level:
- World model: Level 1. Block now at rows 25-29, cols ~24-28 inside vertical corridor after LEFT from cols 29-33. Corridor opening at top (rows 25-29, cols 29-33) is the only entry point - LEFT is blocked from all other vertical positions. Block moves in 5-cell increments.
- Goal model: Move block through corridor to reach white cross area (cols 19-22). Block is now inside corridor and can continue LEFT.
- Recent findings: ['LEFT works only from rows 25-29 (corridor top opening)', 'Block entered corridor at cols 29-33 after UP to rows 25-29 then LEFT', 'Block continues moving LEFT through corridor']
- Open questions: ['How many more LEFT moves to reach white cross?', 'Does block need to reach exact white cross position or just enter corridor?']
- Plan: Continue moving LEFT through corridor until block reaches white cross area or level completes
- Strategy log: ['Abandoned corridor navigation from bottom/middle - LEFT blocked from rows 30-44. Found entry only at top opening rows 25-29.']
- Failed probes: ['LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 35-39: blocked (step 6)', 'LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 40-44: blocked (step 11)', 'LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 30-34: blocked (step 16)', 'RIGHT,LEFT,LEFT sequence: no extra movement (step 18)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 28 actions; a strong score needs about 22 or fewer. You are 1.3x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The RIGHT action didn't move the block - it's still at rows 30-34, cols 14-18. Wait, looking at the segmentation:
- Node 6: color=O, boundary=[[30, 14], [30, 18], [31, 18], [31, 14]] - orange at rows 30-31, cols 14-18
- Node 9: color=b, boundary=[[32, 14], [32, 18], [34, 18], [34, 14]] - blue at rows 32-34, cols 14-18
So the block is at rows 30-34, cols 14-18. The white cross (Node 8) is at [[31, 21], [32, 22], [32, 21]] - rows 31-32, cols 21-22.
Wait, the block moved from cols 19-23 to cols 14-18! So LEFT actually DID work, even though I thought it was blocked. The block is now at rows 30-34, cols 14-18, and the white cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22.
So the block is to the LEFT of the white cross. The white cross is at cols 21-22, and the block is at cols 14-18. If I move RIGHT, the block would move to cols 19-23, which would cover the white cross at cols 21-22.
But wait, I just tried RIGHT and it didn't work. Let me check again... Actually, looking at the result, the board changed (board_changed: true), but the block is at cols 14-18. So the RIGHT action didn't move the block.
Hmm, let me re-examine. The previous state had the block at cols 19-23, and after RIGHT, the block is at cols 14-18. That's a LEFT movement, not RIGHT! So something strange is happening.
Wait, I think I'm confusing the states. Let me trace through:
1. Before the last action, the block was at rows 30-34, cols 19-23 (from the DOWN action).
2. I executed LEFT, which should have moved the block to cols 14-18.
3. Then I executed RIGHT, which should have moved the block back to cols 19-23.
But the segmentation shows the block at cols 14-18 after RIGHT. So RIGHT didn't work from this position.
Actually wait, let me re-read the output. The segmentation was printed BEFORE the RIGHT action was executed. So the block was at cols 14-18 before RIGHT. After RIGHT, the block should be at cols 19-23 if RIGHT worked.
But the image shows the block still at the left position. Let me check the current segmentation to see where the block is now.
Actually, I think the issue is that I printed the segmentation before executing RIGHT, and the print showed the block at cols 14-18. Then RIGHT was executed, and the block should have moved to cols 19-23. But the image shows the block still at the left position.
Wait, looking at the image more carefully, the block is at the left edge of the gray room, and the white cross is to the right of the block. This matches cols 14-18 for the block and cols 21-22 for the white cross.
So RIGHT didn't work from this position. Let me try RIGHT again to see if it works now, or try a different approach.
Actually, I think the issue is that LEFT worked from rows 30-34, contrary to what I thought earlier. The block moved from cols 19-23 to cols 14-18 when I executed LEFT. Now I need to figure out how to get the block to cover the white cross at cols 21-22.
If RIGHT doesn't work from rows 30-34, then I need to move the block to a different row where RIGHT works. Earlier, RIGHT worked from rows 25-29. So I should try UP to move the block to rows 25-29, then RIGHT to move it to cols 19-23, then DOWN to move it back to rows 30-34 to cover the white cross.
But wait, that's a lot of actions. Let me think about this more carefully.
The white cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22. The block is at rows 30-34, cols 14-18. To cover the white cross, the block needs to be at rows 30-34, cols 19-23 (or similar).
If RIGHT doesn't work from rows 30-34, I need to find another way. Let me try UP to move the block to rows 25-29, then RIGHT to move it to cols 19-23, then DOWN to move it back to rows 30-34.
Actually, let me first check if RIGHT works from the current position by trying it again. If it doesn't work, I'll try UP then RIGHT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current block position after RIGHT
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('O', 'b', 'W'):
        print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Block at rows 30-34, cols 14-18. White cross at rows 31-32, cols 21-22.
# RIGHT didn't work from this position. Try UP then RIGHT then DOWN.
# UP moves block to rows 25-29, where RIGHT works.
action(['UP', 'RIGHT', 'DOWN'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves block right by ~5 cols; works from rows 25-29", "LEFT": "Moves block left by ~5 cols; works from rows 25-29 and rows 30-34", "UP": "Moves block up by 5 rows", "DOWN": "Moves block down by 5 rows"}, "object_taxonomy": {"white_cross": "Static reference marker at rows 31-32, cols 21-22", "orange_blue_block": "Movable object (4x2 orange + 5x3 blue)", "dark_gray_corridor": "L-shaped passage inside gray room", "yellow_bar": "Timer"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "Yellow bar + red squares - timer"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Level 1. Block at rows 30-34, cols 14-18. White cross at rows 31-32, cols 21-22. RIGHT blocked from rows 30-34.", "goal_model": "Position block to cover white cross at rows 31-32, cols 21-22", "recent_findings": ["LEFT works from rows 30-34 (contrary to earlier belief)", "RIGHT blocked from rows 30-34"], "open_questions": ["Does UP-RIGHT-DOWN sequence cover white cross?"], "current_plan": "UP to rows 25-29, RIGHT to cols 19-23, DOWN to rows 30-34 to cover white cross", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
